content: Just as language learning reflexion (the core vocabulary learning concept from high school English curricula) requires learners to actively map new word meanings to existing contextual knowledge to solidify understanding, LLM self-reflection agents can be redesigned to incorporate this active vocabulary-aligned reflexion step, where agents explicitly map incorrect prior solution outputs to grounded domain vocabulary semantics to correct reasoning gaps. This creates a new scaffolded self-reflection framework that mirrors human learning routines.
